name: TabPFN (Transformer for Tabular Data)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); AutoML Freiburg; TabPFN Cloud API available

auth.notes: Local inference: no auth; TabPFN Cloud API: API key

performance.notes: Single forward pass — no training loop; inference-time learning; fast for small datasets

agent_readiness.notes: Prior-data Fitted Network for tabular classification. Transformer trained on synthetic datasets to do in-context learning on new tabular data — no gradient-based fine-tuning. sklearn-compatible API. TabPFNClassifier.fit() + predict() like any sklearn model. Beats XGBoost on small tabular datasets (< 10k rows). Single forward pass for prediction. TabPFN v2 extended to regression, larger datasets. Cloud API for larger inference.

gotchas: Designed for small datasets (< 10k rows) — performance degrades on larger
Requires GPU for fast inference (CPU is slow)
Classification only in v1; v2 adds regression
Cannot explain predictions as easily as tree-based models
best_when: Small tabular classification datasets (< 10k rows) needing quick strong baseline
When XGBoost/LightGBM underperform on small datasets
AutoML pipelines needing fast powerful tabular models
summary: TabPFN is a Transformer pre-trained on synthetic data to do in-context learning on new tabular datasets — no training required. sklearn-compatible API (fit + predict). Beats XGBoost on small tabular classification datasets (< 10k rows) without hyperparameter tuning. Single forward pass for predictions. Cloud API for larger datasets. TabPFN v2 extends to regression. Game-changer for small dataset ML baselines.
